paper_title: Induced 2-degenerate Subgraphs of Triangle-free Planar Graphs
summary: Dvořák and Kelly prove $\alpha_2(G) \ge \frac{4}{5}n$ for triangle-free planar graphs and informally believe the same argument can be pushed to $\frac{5}{6}$, but they do not pursue it because it would not suffice to prove the full $\frac{7}{8}$ conjecture. No subsequent paper has been found that establishes the $\frac{5}{6}$ bound or otherwise resolves this informal belief; a 2025 survey of degenerate induced subgraphs in sparse graphs (arXiv:2511.13693) cites the $\frac{4}{5}$ result without improvement.
search_queries: arXiv 1709.04036 Dvorak Kelly induced 2-degenerate subgraphs planar citing papers
notes: The 5/6 claim is an informal belief stated in the paper, not a formally labelled conjecture. A 2025 paper (arXiv:2511.13693, 'A Note on Large Degenerate Induced Subgraphs in Sparse Graphs') cites the Dvořák–Kelly 4/5 result but does not improve upon it for the triangle-free planar case. The full 7/8 conjecture (Conjecture 1.1 of the source paper) also remains open.
